alt: Walnut floor refinishing project at Bing Crosby Ranch in San Diego, one of four documented photographs from this hardwood floor refinishing job completed by San Diego Hardwoods, reflecting the true 100% dust-containment sanding and hardwood floor refinishing work performed throughout San Diego County.

Alternate view of walnut hardwood flooring during the Bing Crosby Ranch refinishing project in San Diego, completed by San Diego Hardwoods with true 100% dust-containment sanding and hardwood floor refinishing service throughout San Diego County.

Walnut flooring from the Bing Crosby Ranch refinishing project in San Diego, documenting the same hardwood floor job completed by San Diego Hardwoods using true 100% dust-containment sanding and hardwood floor refinishing service throughout San Diego County.

Additional view of the Bing Crosby Ranch walnut floor project in San Diego, part of this hardwood floor refinishing job completed by San Diego Hardwoods with true 100% dust containment throughout San Diego County.

Vintage random-width American cherry flooring restored in an expansive Encinitas home, seen here from the kitchen island looking into the dining room. Decades of fading, wax, scratches and wear were corrected through selective sanding, deep cleaning, color matching, clear sealing and two coats of Bona Traffic HD in a commercial satin sheen.
caption: Vintage random-width American cherry flooring restored in an expansive Encinitas home, seen here from the kitchen island looking into the dining room. Decades of fading, wax, scratches and wear were corrected through selective sanding, deep cleaning, color matching, clear sealing and two coats of Bona Traffic HD in a commercial satin sheen.

alt: A second completed view of the Encinitas American cherry restoration, from the coffered-ceiling dining room looking toward the kitchen and stone fireplace. The worn, wax-contaminated floor was selectively sanded, cleaned, matched to the surrounding cherry and protected with a clear Bona sealer and commercial-satin Traffic HD finish.
caption: A second completed view of the Encinitas American cherry restoration, from the coffered-ceiling dining room looking toward the kitchen and stone fireplace. The worn, wax-contaminated floor was selectively sanded, cleaned, matched to the surrounding cherry and protected with a clear Bona sealer and commercial-satin Traffic HD finish.

alt: Four-inch solid maple flooring restored in a Point Loma residence, with the great-room windows framing the marina, downtown skyline and San Diego Bay. Heavy sun fading, wax, polish, scratches and wear were removed before the floor received a modern creamy tone and an extra-durable matte water-based finish.
caption: Four-inch solid maple flooring restored in a Point Loma residence, with the great-room windows framing the marina, downtown skyline and San Diego Bay. Heavy sun fading, wax, polish, scratches and wear were removed before the floor received a modern creamy tone and an extra-durable matte water-based finish.

alt: Another completed view of the Point Loma maple project, seen from the staircase with the terrace doors open to the bay. Precision sanding and planetary refinement produced a flat surface, while the selected creamy tone and matte commercial-grade finish gave the decades-old floor a clean modern appearance.
caption: Another completed view of the Point Loma maple project, seen from the staircase with the terrace doors open to the bay. Precision sanding and planetary refinement produced a flat surface, while the selected creamy tone and matte commercial-grade finish gave the decades-old floor a clean modern appearance.

alt: Final view of the restored Point Loma solid maple flooring in the dining area with its mirrored wall and chandelier. The original floor was cleaned, decontaminated and sanded with full dust containment before receiving the homeowners' chosen creamy tone and a durable matte finish.
caption: Final view of the restored Point Loma solid maple flooring in the dining area with its mirrored wall and chandelier. The original floor was cleaned, decontaminated and sanded with full dust containment before receiving the homeowners' chosen creamy tone and a durable matte finish.

alt: Vintage Graff Brothers rift-and-quartered white oak restored in a coastal Bird Rock home. The floors and stairs were sanded with double-HEPA dust containment, sealed clear to preserve the natural white-oak color and protected with a commercial-grade satin water-based finish.
caption: Vintage Graff Brothers rift-and-quartered white oak restored in a coastal Bird Rock home. The floors and stairs were sanded with double-HEPA dust containment, sealed clear to preserve the natural white-oak color and protected with a commercial-grade satin water-based finish.

alt: Wide-plank character hickory restored in a Carmel Valley home, seen along the hallway beneath the wrought-iron chandelier. After cupping, water damage, scratches, fading and heavy ambering, the floor was sanded flat, clear sealed and finished with a matte Bona water-based polyurethane.
caption: Wide-plank character hickory restored in a Carmel Valley home, seen along the hallway beneath the wrought-iron chandelier. After cupping, water damage, scratches, fading and heavy ambering, the floor was sanded flat, clear sealed and finished with a matte Bona water-based polyurethane.

alt: A second completed view of the Carmel Valley hickory project in the great room with its stone fireplace and French doors, showing the natural variation of the random-width character planks after precision sanding, clear sealing and matte Bona protection.
caption: A second completed view of the Carmel Valley hickory project in the great room with its stone fireplace and French doors, showing the natural variation of the random-width character planks after precision sanding, clear sealing and matte Bona protection.

alt: Five-inch maple flooring transformed in a Del Mar ocean-view home, seen from the front entry beneath the vaulted plank ceiling with the glass stair rail at right and the ocean beyond the sliders. After sanding to raw wood and filling gaps and cracks, the floor received a white stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw for a soft, ultra-low-sheen finish.
caption: Five-inch maple flooring transformed in a Del Mar ocean-view home, seen from the front entry beneath the vaulted plank ceiling with the glass stair rail at right and the ocean beyond the sliders. After sanding to raw wood and filling gaps and cracks, the floor received a white stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw for a soft, ultra-low-sheen finish.

alt: Solid ash flooring overlooking Torrey Pines Beach restored after years of wax, polish, sun, water and coastal exposure. The 45-degree floor was cleaned, sanded flat and finished clear so the natural ash grain remained the focus.
caption: Solid ash flooring overlooking Torrey Pines Beach restored after years of wax, polish, sun, water and coastal exposure. The 45-degree floor was cleaned, sanded flat and finished clear so the natural ash grain remained the focus.

alt: Engineered red oak restored throughout a split-level residence. A custom stain was selected to emphasize the red-oak grain, followed by a durable matte Bona Traffic finish.
caption: Engineered red oak restored throughout a split-level residence. A custom stain was selected to emphasize the red-oak grain, followed by a durable matte Bona Traffic finish.

alt: New red oak flooring was installed to match the existing floor in this Carmel Valley–Del Mar home. The entire surface was then sanded, stained in a custom provincial tone and finished together for a consistent result.
caption: New red oak flooring was installed to match the existing floor in this Carmel Valley–Del Mar home. The entire surface was then sanded, stained in a custom provincial tone and finished together for a consistent result.

alt: The final coat is still wet on this expansive Rancho Santa Fe entryway. Vintage 2¼-inch red oak, likely dating to the 1960s, was restored in a natural color and protected with a fresh finish.
caption: The final coat is still wet on this expansive Rancho Santa Fe entryway. Vintage 2¼-inch red oak, likely dating to the 1960s, was restored in a natural color and protected with a fresh finish.

alt: The final finish coat is still wet on the engineered Brazilian cherry floor in this First Avenue Coronado residence overlooking San Diego Bay. Reflections from the custom lighting and millwork show the freshly restored surface.
caption: The final finish coat is still wet on the engineered Brazilian cherry floor in this First Avenue Coronado residence overlooking San Diego Bay. Reflections from the custom lighting and millwork show the freshly restored surface.

alt: Traditional white oak flooring restored in a North Park home. The floor was returned to a natural color, sealed with an oil sealer and protected with a residential-grade water-based finish.
caption: Traditional white oak flooring restored in a North Park home. The floor was returned to a natural color, sealed with an oil sealer and protected with a residential-grade water-based finish.

alt: New engineered red oak was installed in one room and matched to the existing floor in this Encinitas–Carlsbad home. Clear sealer and clear finish preserved a restrained, natural red-oak appearance.
caption: New engineered red oak was installed in one room and matched to the existing floor in this Encinitas–Carlsbad home. Clear sealer and clear finish preserved a restrained, natural red-oak appearance.

alt: Custom rift-and-quartered red oak restored in a Fairbanks Ranch residence, seen along the hallway leading up to the front-entry steps framed by the home's original dark millwork. The decades-old dark floor was transformed with a creamy beige treatment that retained the architectural grain while creating a lighter modern interior.
caption: Custom rift-and-quartered red oak restored in a Fairbanks Ranch residence, seen along the hallway leading up to the front-entry steps framed by the home's original dark millwork. The decades-old dark floor was transformed with a creamy beige treatment that retained the architectural grain while creating a lighter modern interior.

alt: Twenty color options were applied directly to this vintage Mission Hills white oak floor so the homeowners could compare natural, stained and toned finishes before making a final selection.
caption: Twenty color options were applied directly to this vintage Mission Hills white oak floor so the homeowners could compare natural, stained and toned finishes before making a final selection.

alt: Traditional two-inch No. 1-grade red oak strip flooring being installed in an Oceanside home. The boards are being nailed down over tar paper as part of the new hardwood-floor installation.
caption: Traditional two-inch No. 1-grade red oak strip flooring being installed in an Oceanside home. The boards are being nailed down over tar paper as part of the new hardwood-floor installation.

alt: Engineered walnut flooring restored high above downtown San Diego in the Omni Hotel near the Gaslamp District. Sunlight through the windows highlights the renewed walnut floor with the Coronado Bridge and Hilton Bayfront beyond.
caption: Engineered walnut flooring restored high above downtown San Diego in the Omni Hotel near the Gaslamp District. Sunlight through the windows highlights the renewed walnut floor with the Coronado Bridge and Hilton Bayfront beyond.

alt: A second completed view of the Del Mar five-inch maple project, seen from the upper stair looking down over the living room, fireplace and deck doors. White stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw created a clean, modern floor with an ultra-low sheen that complements the ocean-view home.
caption: A second completed view of the Del Mar five-inch maple project, seen from the upper stair looking down over the living room, fireplace and deck doors. White stain and white-tinted Bona Traffic HD Raw created a clean, modern floor with an ultra-low sheen that complements the ocean-view home.

alt: Original Douglas fir uncovered beneath carpet in a historic late-1800s Golden Hill apartment building. The vintage floor was restored with a dark Jacobean tone and a commercial-grade finish for durable rental use.
caption: Original Douglas fir uncovered beneath carpet in a historic late-1800s Golden Hill apartment building. The vintage floor was restored with a dark Jacobean tone and a commercial-grade finish for durable rental use.

alt: Oak flooring maintained in a multi-level La Jolla Shores home overlooking the ocean. The floor was deep cleaned, de-waxed and recoated without sanding so the existing wear layer and subtle amber tone could be preserved.
caption: Oak flooring maintained in a multi-level La Jolla Shores home overlooking the ocean. The floor was deep cleaned, de-waxed and recoated without sanding so the existing wear layer and subtle amber tone could be preserved.

alt: Mid-century red oak restored in a La Jolla short-term rental near Bird Rock and La Jolla Cove. The completed gray-stain finish reflects the contemporary color direction selected for this property.
caption: Mid-century red oak restored in a La Jolla short-term rental near Bird Rock and La Jolla Cove. The completed gray-stain finish reflects the contemporary color direction selected for this property.

alt: Engineered Brazilian cherry installed at a 45-degree angle was transformed in this Solana Beach home. The floor was sanded, bleached three times, stained off-white and finished in a low sheen to reduce the original red color.
caption: Engineered Brazilian cherry installed at a 45-degree angle was transformed in this Solana Beach home. The floor was sanded, bleached three times, stained off-white and finished in a low sheen to reduce the original red color.

alt: After reviewing numerous samples, the homeowners selected a natural finish for this expansive vintage white oak floor on the west side of Mission Hills. Precision sanding and a durable clear finish returned the original strip flooring to a clean, timeless appearance.
caption: After reviewing numerous samples, the homeowners selected a natural finish for this expansive vintage white oak floor on the west side of Mission Hills. Precision sanding and a durable clear finish returned the original strip flooring to a clean, timeless appearance.

alt: Floating maple stairs restored at the Ionis Pharmaceuticals office in Carlsbad. The finish is still wet, and the specialty work was completed while protecting the stainless-steel stringers and plexiglass components.
caption: Floating maple stairs restored at the Ionis Pharmaceuticals office in Carlsbad. The finish is still wet, and the specialty work was completed while protecting the stainless-steel stringers and plexiglass components.

alt: A second view of the restored Carlsbad office staircase while the maple finish is still wet. A brown safety line was added at the lower step as part of the completed commercial project.
caption: A second view of the restored Carlsbad office staircase while the maple finish is still wet. A brown safety line was added at the lower step as part of the completed commercial project.

alt: Restoration underway on a 1970s Del Mar white oak floor that had turned heavily orange and suffered termite damage. The belt sander is working down the middle of the room, connected to dust containment, as damaged boards are replaced and the floor is taken back to raw wood.
caption: Restoration underway on a 1970s Del Mar white oak floor that had turned heavily orange and suffered termite damage. The belt sander is working down the middle of the room, connected to dust containment, as damaged boards are replaced and the floor is taken back to raw wood.

alt: Sealer and stain options tested directly on the sanded Del Mar white oak floor, with the family dog inspecting the work beside the open balcony door and the ocean beyond the rooftops. The homeowners ultimately selected a restrained clear-white treatment.
caption: Sealer and stain options tested directly on the sanded Del Mar white oak floor, with the family dog inspecting the work beside the open balcony door and the ocean beyond the rooftops. The homeowners ultimately selected a restrained clear-white treatment.

alt: A second sample view from the Del Mar white oak project, with each option written directly on the raw oak for side-by-side comparison before the final subtle-white selection.
caption: A second sample view from the Del Mar white oak project, with each option written directly on the raw oak for side-by-side comparison before the final subtle-white selection.

alt: Completed Del Mar white oak restoration in the corner room wrapped by French doors and windows, with the curved balcony, palms and ocean beyond. The formerly orange, damaged floor now reads clean, light and modern with a subtle clear-white tone under Bona Traffic HD satin.
caption: Completed Del Mar white oak restoration in the corner room wrapped by French doors and windows, with the curved balcony, palms and ocean beyond. The formerly orange, damaged floor now reads clean, light and modern with a subtle clear-white tone under Bona Traffic HD satin.

alt: A second completed view of the Del Mar white oak project, looking across the living area toward the balcony doors. After board replacement, full sanding and a restrained white treatment, satin Bona Traffic HD protects the restored strip floor.
caption: A second completed view of the Del Mar white oak project, looking across the living area toward the balcony doors. After board replacement, full sanding and a restrained white treatment, satin Bona Traffic HD protects the restored strip floor.

alt: Rare solid Brazilian cherry flooring restored at the top of Mount Soledad in La Jolla. The 2¼-inch floor and matching solid stair treads and risers were refinished while preserving the natural reds, oranges and yellows of the wood.
caption: Rare solid Brazilian cherry flooring restored at the top of Mount Soledad in La Jolla. The 2¼-inch floor and matching solid stair treads and risers were refinished while preserving the natural reds, oranges and yellows of the wood.

alt: Vintage red oak transformed in a Rancho Santa Fe Farms home, seen from the living room looking down the hall to the French doors. Multiple translucent layers of creamy-white sealer softened the reds and yellows while allowing the original grain and color variation to remain visible.
caption: Vintage red oak transformed in a Rancho Santa Fe Farms home, seen from the living room looking down the hall to the French doors. Multiple translucent layers of creamy-white sealer softened the reds and yellows while allowing the original grain and color variation to remain visible.

alt: Another completed view of the Rancho Santa Fe Farms red oak floor, in the room with the white built-in display shelving, showing the creamy Scandinavian overlay while the natural red-oak grain remains visible.
caption: Another completed view of the Rancho Santa Fe Farms red oak floor, in the room with the white built-in display shelving, showing the creamy Scandinavian overlay while the natural red-oak grain remains visible.

caption: The nook area before restoration, showing the original red oak floor prior to sanding, sampling and the creamy Scandinavian transformation.

alt: The same nook after the red oak was sanded to raw wood, with color samples applied directly to the floor before the final creamy-white selection.
caption: The same nook after the red oak was sanded to raw wood, with color samples applied directly to the floor before the final creamy-white selection.

alt: The finished nook after the red oak was transformed with a translucent creamy-white treatment, creating a light Scandinavian look while retaining visible wood grain.
caption: The finished nook after the red oak was transformed with a translucent creamy-white treatment, creating a light Scandinavian look while retaining visible wood grain.

alt: Completed Fallbrook red oak transformation in the bay-window sitting room, where a picture-frame border follows the room's angles. At least three layers of creamy-white sealer and multiple matte coats of Bona Traffic HD created a smooth, light and highly protected floor.
caption: Completed Fallbrook red oak transformation in the bay-window sitting room, where a picture-frame border follows the room's angles. At least three layers of creamy-white sealer and multiple matte coats of Bona Traffic HD created a smooth, light and highly protected floor.

alt: Another completed perspective of the Fallbrook restoration, looking through the French doors toward the brick-fireplace family room. Vintage red oak was transformed with layered creamy-white sealer and multiple matte coats of commercial-grade Bona finish.
caption: Another completed perspective of the Fallbrook restoration, looking through the French doors toward the brick-fireplace family room. Vintage red oak was transformed with layered creamy-white sealer and multiple matte coats of commercial-grade Bona finish.

alt: Wide-plank French oak restored in an Encinitas home, seen across the open-plan great room toward the sliders and patio. The substantial engineered wear layer allowed precision sanding, followed by a custom beige stain and additional matte protection for the completed floor.
caption: Wide-plank French oak restored in an Encinitas home, seen across the open-plan great room toward the sliders and patio. The substantial engineered wear layer allowed precision sanding, followed by a custom beige stain and additional matte protection for the completed floor.

alt: Completed Bird Rock white oak restoration showing the natural rift-and-quartered grain after dust-contained sanding, clear sealing and a satin commercial-grade finish.
caption: Completed Bird Rock white oak restoration showing the natural rift-and-quartered grain after dust-contained sanding, clear sealing and a satin commercial-grade finish.

alt: The completed Bird Rock white oak staircase viewed from above, showing the restored treads, open ends, spindles and lower landing after precision sanding and finishing.
caption: The completed Bird Rock white oak staircase viewed from above, showing the restored treads, open ends, spindles and lower landing after precision sanding and finishing.

alt: New rift-and-quartered select white oak installed in a Bankers Hill home, seen from the kitchen looking into the wainscoted dining and living rooms. The floor was custom stained in a modern creamy-brown tone and protected with a durable Bona water-based finish.
